meta.description: Lightweight, modular template engine that processes any file type using Handlebars.js and JSON data

project-definition.what-is-minty: Minty is a UNIVERSAL TEMPLATE ENGINE, not just a static site generator
project-definition.core-concept: Process ANY file type (HTML, CSS, JSON, Markdown, etc.) as templates using Handlebars.js
project-definition.key-distinction: Unlike static site generators that focus on HTML/websites, Minty can generate ANY file type from templates
project-definition.not-limited-to: websites or HTML - can process any text-based file format
project-definition.primary-strength: universal file type support with template processing

config.purpose: Load and validate .mintyrc configuration file from parent directory

data.purpose: Load and validate JSON data file containing template content

templates.purpose: Discover template and partial files with configured extensions

renderer.purpose: Compile Handlebars templates and merge with data

partials.purpose: Process and substitute partial template includes

files.purpose: Handle file operations - clear, copy, write

main.purpose: Main module exports for programmatic use

wildcards.description: Generate multiple files from single template using asterisk pattern
wildcards.behavior: Loop through sub-keys and generate separate files

partials.behavior: Replace import with rendered partial content

extensions.description: Process any file type as templates

processing.validation: Check data availability for each template
processing.substitution: Replace partial imports with rendered content
processing.output: Write final files to dist directory

ai-guidance.CRITICAL: READ THIS SECTION FIRST - Contains essential AI instructions
ai-guidance.mandatory-behavior: ALWAYS consult this AI context file before making assumptions about the project
ai-guidance.remember: Minty is a UNIVERSAL TEMPLATE ENGINE - always refer to it correctly
when-to-consult: when user asks about project capabilities
ai-guidance.emphasize: Minty processes ANY file type, not just websites
ai-guidance.when-explaining: Always mention universal file support as key differentiator
ai-guidance.verification-step: Before responding, ask yourself: Am I describing Minty as a universal template engine?

ai-context.purpose: Optimized project representation for AI understanding and evolution
ai-context.updates: automatic during builds and manual via CLI

ai-protocol.step-3: Review 'ai-guidance' for terminology and behavior rules
ai-protocol.step-5: Verify your understanding matches the context before responding
ai-protocol.enforcement: If you catch yourself making assumptions, STOP and consult this file
ai-protocol.success-indicator: You correctly identify Minty as a universal template engine, not just a static site generator

testing-standards.ABSOLUTE-REQUIREMENT: 100% CODE COVERAGE IS MANDATORY - NO EXCEPTIONS

coverage-policy.statements: MUST be exactly 100% - anything less is FAILURE
coverage-policy.branches: MUST be exactly 100% - anything less is FAILURE
coverage-policy.functions: MUST be exactly 100% - anything less is FAILURE
coverage-policy.lines: MUST be exactly 100% - anything less is FAILURE
coverage-policy.acceptance-criteria: ALL four metrics must reach 100% simultaneously

AI-MUST-NEVER: Mark task as complete without 100% coverage
AI-MUST-ALWAYS: Persist until 100% coverage is achieved on ALL metrics
Find creative solutions to test difficult code paths
Refactor code if necessary to make it testable
Test error conditions and exception paths
Verify every single line of code is executed in tests
Keep trying different approaches until 100% is reached

file-naming-conventions.enforcement: Any test file without .spec.js extension is WRONG
file-naming-conventions.location: All tests MUST be in tests/ directory

forbidden-files.rationale: These files are unnecessary clutter - tests should be self-documenting

test-structure.mock-strategy: Use jest.unstable_mockModule for all external dependencies
test-structure.isolation: Each test must be completely isolated with beforeEach/afterEach
test-structure.coverage-requirements: Every function, branch, statement, and line must be tested

edge-cases-required: Regex edge cases and malformed patterns

success-definition.verification: npm test must exit with code 0 and show 100% in coverage report
success-definition.reporting: Coverage table must show 100 | 100 | 100 | 100 for all files
success-definition.NO-SUCCESS-IF: Any metric is 99.x% or lower, even if all tests pass

if-coverage-below-100: Immediately identify which lines/branches are not covered
Read the source code to understand why they're not covered
Create specific tests to cover those exact lines
Try different input combinations to trigger uncovered branches
Mock dependencies differently to reach uncovered code paths
Refactor tests if current approach cannot reach 100%
NEVER-say: The remaining lines are not important
We can skip these edge cases
AI-behavior-on-coverage-gaps.persistence-required: Continue trying new approaches indefinitely until 100% is reached

quality-over-quantity.principle: Every test must be meaningful and test actual behavior
quality-over-quantity.avoid: Tests that only call functions without assertions
quality-over-quantity.ensure: Tests verify correct output, error handling, and side effects
quality-over-quantity.coverage-means: Every line is not only executed but properly tested

AI-accountability.responsibility: AI is 100% responsible for achieving 100% coverage
AI-accountability.no-excuses: There is no valid reason to accept less than 100%
AI-accountability.mindset: 100% or nothing - partial success does not exist

change-management.every-change: Every new feature, bugfix, or configuration change MUST include at least one automated test that proves the behavior and keeps coverage at 100%
scope: exercise both happy paths and failure paths introduced by the change
update existing test suites whenever the behavior is modified
block commits or merges unless npm test/yarn test reports 100% on all metrics
change-management.pull-requests: Update the PR description to highlight which new or adjusted tests safeguard the change
change-management.documentation: If the change exposes new capabilities, update README.md or relevant docs after ensuring the tests are in place
change-management.enforcement: If it is not possible to create tests immediately, the change MUST NOT proceed until full coverage is guaranteed
